The '''[[Wigan Warriors]]''' play [[Rugby League]] in [[Wigan]], [[England]]. Their 2018 season results in the [[Super League XXIII]] and the [[2018 Challenge Cup]] are shown below.

==Preseason friendlies==
Wigan had a pre-season tour in Australia ahead of hosting a [[2018 Super League season|Super League]] game at [[Wollongong Showground]].<ref>https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/02/26/all-2018-nrl-pre-season-trial-results/amp/?amp_gsa=1&amp_js_v=a9&usqp=mq331AQIUAKwASCAAgM%3D#amp_tf=From%20%251%24s&aoh=17712464118240&referrer=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.google.com</ref>

==Super League==
[[File:WIN Stadium Panorama From Western Stand.jpg|thumb|300px|Before kick-off at the [[Wollongong Showground]] between Wigan and [[Hull F.C.]] - the first Super League game to be played in Australia]]
{{main|Super League XXIII}}
Wigan generally kept to consistent good form throughout the 2018 league season. They won all seven Super 8s games and sixteen out of twenty-three in the regular season. The Warriors went on to finish the season second.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/teams/wigan/scores-fixtures|title=Wigan Warriors - Scores & Fixtures - Rugby League|website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/competitions/super-league/results/2018-19 | title=Super League Results | date=October 2018 | publisher=Sky Sports | accessdate=27 December 2020 }}</ref>

===Grand Final Playoffs===
Wigan finished second in the league, qualifying them for the Grand Final Playoffs. Wigan beat [[Castleford Tigers]] in the semifinals before beating [[Warrington Wolves]], who had knocked them out of the [[2018 Challenge Cup|Challenge Cup]] in the summer, to claim their fifth Grand Final championship.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/45800241|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan claim fifth title with victory over Warrington|work=BBC Sport|date=11 October 2018}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2018/oct/13/super-league-grand-final-2018-wigan-warriors-v-warrington-wolves-live|title=Super League Grand Final 2018: Wigan 12-4 Warrington – as it happened|first=James|last=Piercy|newspaper=The Guardian|date=13 October 2018|via=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>

==Challenge Cup==
{{main|2018 Challenge Cup}}
As a [[Super League XXII|2017 Super League]] Super 8s team, Wigan Warriors entered the [[2018 Challenge Cup]] in the sixth round and were drawn against [[Hull Kingston Rovers|Hull KR]].<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/43881309|title=Challenge Cup sixth round: Holders Hull FC drawn away to Featherstone Rovers|work=BBC Sport|date=25 April 2018}}</ref> Wigan beat Hull KR and progress to the quarterfinals where they were knocked out by [[Warrington Wolves]].<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/44291519|title=Challenge Cup: Warrington Wolves v Wigan Warriors|work=BBC Sport|date=31 May 2018}}</ref>
